page: Amazon Web Services r6in.xlarge is a memory-optimized instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it features 4 dedicated vCPUs derived from 2 physical cores with hyperthreading enabled. The instance is equipped with 32.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It utilizes the AWS Nitro hypervisor and does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage.

Benchmark data reveals a contrast between single-core and multi-core performance. Single-core metrics, such as stress-ng and Geekbench, fall within the average range, supported by a 54 MB L3 cache and instruction sets like AVX-512 and cryptography acceleration. However, multi-core performance, including Geekbench multi-core and stress-ng multi-core, is positioned in the lower 25th percentile. Memory bandwidth benchmarks for both cached and uncached operations also rank in the bottom 25th percentile, though the Passmark memory score remains average.

The instance provides a baseline network bandwidth of 6 Gbps. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r6in.xlarge offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio suitable for memory-bound workloads that do not require intense multi-threaded compute or high local storage IOPS. Tradeoffs include lower multi-core processing efficiency and uncached memory bandwidth. It is best suited for workloads such as in-memory caching, static web serving, and moderate database operations.
description: Amazon Web Services r6in.xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs and 32.0 GB of DDR4 RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 8375C processor at 3.5 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture and the Nitro hypervisor, this instance offers an 8.0 GB memory-per-core ratio but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks indicate average single-core capabilities, supported by AVX-512 and cryptographic hardware acceleration, alongside average static web serving throughput of 6.36 GB/sec. However, multi-core processing and uncached memory bandwidth fall into the lower percentiles. With a baseline network bandwidth of 6 Gbps, the r6in.xlarge is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-intensive applications that do not demand heavy parallel computing, making it a suitable fit for in-memory caching, moderate database operations, and web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r6in.xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 4 vCPUs, 32.0 GB of DDR4 RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8375C processor, designed for memory-intensive workloads without local storage.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring a high RAM-to-vCPU ratio and dedicated Intel Xeon processing for memory-intensive workloads.

Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz
6 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with Nitro hypervisor
No local storage or GPU hardware included
Best suited for in-memory caching, web serving, and moderate database tasks
